definitions:
The following definitions shall have the meanings hereby respectively assigned to them.
“Business Hours”: Monday through Friday from 8:00 to 18:00 CET/CEST excluding Belgian official holidays.
“Log Source” : device or system which generates Security Events
“Horizon” : Customer Portal provided by Cegeka for reporting and change management
“Initial Priority” : the priority which is pre-assigned automaticity to a Security Alert specific to a technology.
“SOC/CSIRT Analyst” : Member of the SOC performing Incident Response activities
“Security Event” : data recorded by a security system which represent security related occurrence, such as a failed or successful login attempt, allowed connection through a firewall, …
“Security Alert” :  A notification of a potential security anomaly violating security policies.​
“Security Incident ” :  A Security Alert that has been triaged and has indications of malicious or unwanted activities which requires additional investigation.​
“Confirmed Security Incident” : A Security Incident that has been investigated and verified to include malicious or unwanted activities.​
“Service ” : Managed Detection & Response services

transition services:
Transition Services consists of two (2) types of activities:
These two (2) activities form a consistent approach in completing the transition of the MDR Service towards Run Services. The start of the Transition Services begins when the contract is signed and ends when the “Ready for Service” requirements are met which are described below.
During the Transition Services Cegeka will not perform Security Alerts / Security Incident handling.
technical implementation:
The goal of the technical implementation is to implement all technical measures as stated in the Statement of Work.
Cegeka will perform the following activities during the technical implementation:
For Cegeka to be able to effectively perform the technical implementation, the customer is responsible for:
The technical implementation will be formalized on a per project base in a Technical Design document. This document will include all the technical decisions related to the Service.
operational readiness:
The goal of Operational Readiness is that both customer and Cegeka have a clear understanding of the governance and operational processes related to the Managed Detection & Response service.
Cegeka will perform the following activities during the Operational Readiness:
The Operational Readiness will be formalized on a per project basis in a Customer Scoping Document . This document will include all the operational agreements related to the Service.
The Customer is responsible for informing Cegeka of any changes which may impact the Service.
rfs - ready-for-service:
The RFS indicates that all the prerequisites are completed during the transition period to start the Service. This is the moment Cegeka will perform the Managed Services on the full contracted scope and invoicing will start accordingly.
The prerequisites for the RFS are:
Prerequisites
To reduce the operational pressure for the customer, a one-month learning period is applied after the start of the RFS date to fine-tune the environment to reduce the number of false positives. This implies that all Security Alerts will only be handled during Business Hours until the learning period has ended, and no SLAs are measured. After this period, the Service will go into full operational mode including all SLA.
The RFS date has a maximum term of three (3) months after signing the contract unless explicitly agreed otherwise.
managed services:
Cegeka works with a standardized “Security Incident Response Plan (S-IRP)” which describes how Cegeka handles information Security Incidents as part of the Managed Detection & Response Service to Customers.
Every occurrence of a Security Alert potentially resulting in a Security Incident is handled logically as described in this plan. The assignment of roles & responsibilities as well as the services and products available to Cegeka within a specific context will determine which parts of the plan will be executed by Cegeka, its customer or 3 rd parties mandated by either or both.
This document describes the entire process, but the contractual agreements and Security Customer Scoping Document (S-CSD) will determine which of the process phases and activities will be handled by Cegeka under which conditions.
High-level representation of the S-IRP
The S-IRP consist of the following steps:
Included in Technical Implementation & Operational Readiness
Included in Advanced Threat Intelligence , Threat Hunting & 24x7 Security Monitoring
Included in Threat Response
Only follow up and support are part of this service.
Only follow up and support are part of this service.
Included in Cyber Security Advisory
advanced threat intelligence:
Our SOC platform incorporated Advanced Threat Intelligence feeds by default. This helps improve real-time detection of the latest emerging threats and a more effective investigation of Security Alerts/Incidents.
Based upon the nature and type of threat, the following activities will be done to improve detection:
Based upon the nature and type of Security Alerts/Incidents, the following enrichments will be done:
threat hunting:
On a monthly basis a threat hunter will proactively search through Security Events to find suspicious, or risky activities that have evaded detection by existing tools. Threat hunting will also consider historical data when searching for anomalies/outliers.
Cegeka will perform the following activities regarding Threat Hunting:
24x7 security monitoring:
The goal of Security Monitoring is to continuously monitor the customer’s environment based upon the scoped technologies and uncover potential security incidents which need to be act upon.
Cegeka will perform the following activities regarding Security Monitoring:
For Cegeka to able to effectively perform Security Monitoring, the customer is responsible for:
The priority of each Security Incident will be determined according to urgency and impact of the Security Incident. Based upon those two factors, the appropriated priority will be assigned accordingly:
URGENCY

The priority can change during the Security Incident Response Process.
Each Security Incident will be classified with an Area to represent the type of Security Incident which best suits the events seen during our investigations.
Cegeka uses 17 standardized Security Incident Areas to classify Security Incidents:
Databreach
Definition
Accidental or purposeful disclosure of information related involving personal data
Examples
Disclosure of information
Definition
Accidental or purposeful disclosure of information NOT containing any personal data
Examples
Loss or theft of equipment
Definition
Loss or theft of any device/medium that contains Customer-related data
Examples
Reconnaissance
Definition
Reconnaissance activities which have been observed on a device or the network. Reconnaissance activities are typically low-interaction and relying upon normal operation of a device/network and the information it discloses. Typically, no malicious payload is sent.
Examples
Malware
Definition
Infection of a device with malicious software
Examples
Technical intrusion
Definition
(Possibility of) access to data or devices without authorization, possibly followed by malicious activities.
This incident area is also used whenever a combination of more than one incident type is identified.
Examples
Policy violation
Definition
An end-user has performed an action that is against the Customer’s security policy
Examples
Denial-Of-Service
Definition
Purposeful disruption or degradation of IT services
Examples
Vulnerability
Definition
Detection of presence/exploitation of a known vulnerability
Examples
Social engineering
Definition
Detection of (attempted) attacks on information systems by use of non-technical or low-technology means - such as lies, impersonation, tricks, bribes, blackmail, and threats.
Used for each such detection except phishing, for which a separate incident area exists
Examples
Probing
Definition
Detection of attempts of sending actual payloads to a system with the objective to learn something about the system or exploit the system. Probing often follows initial reconnaissance.
Examples
Brute forcing
Definition
Detection of activities using a cryptanalysis technique or other kind of attack method involving an exhaustive procedure that tries all possibilities, one-by-one.
Examples
Compliance
Definition
Detection of a technical non-compliance with security baselines
Examples
Phishing
Definition
Report of e-mail with malicious content
Examples
Identity theft
Definition
Detection of events indicating someone wrongfully obtains and uses another person’s personal data in some way that involves fraud or deception
Examples
SOC
Definition
A yet to be categorized security incident to be handled by Cegeka SOC. This incident area is used for monitoring alerts
on security related services. Tickets should never be closed with this area selected.
Examples
N/A
Other
Definition
A security incident that does not fit into any of the other Security Incident Area
Examples
N/A – all known Security Incident Area should fit into one of the other areas at the time of writing
The Security Incident Area can change during the Security Incident Response Process.

The Initial Notification will be done once the Security Incident Ticket has been created and will be communicated towards the Operational Contact(s) which is defined in the Customer Scoping document .
The Security Incident Updates are pro-active notifications from our SOC given to the customer on a recurring base depending on the priority of the Security Incident. These notifications will start once a Security Incident Ticket has been created and will stop when a containment action is performed, or advice is given.
threat response:
The goal of Threat Response is to contain an active Security Incident as to reduce further impact in the customer’s environment. A threat Response only includes containment actions and does not include any activities related to eradicating malicious files or recovering impacted systems.
Containment actions must always be approved by the customer before Cegeka can execute the recommended actions. The approval   can either be “pre-approved” or ad-hoc.
Based upon the nature of the Security Incident, the following response action(s) can be executed in the customer’s environment.
Action
Description
REQUIREMENTS
Host Responses
Isolate Host
Restricting network access to and from the host using an EDR platform
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Terminate Process
Terminate Process on the host using an EDR platform
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Quarantine file on host
Quarantine file on the host using the EDR platform
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Isolate Pod on host
Restricting network access to and from the pod using an EDR platform
MS Defender for Containers
Terminate Pod on host
Terminate POD on the host using an EDR platform
MS Defender for Containers
Identity Responses
Reset user password
This will prompt the user to change their password on the next logon, ensuring that this account can't be used for further impersonation attempts
MS Defender for Identity
Disable user in Active Directory
This will temporarily prevent an AD user from signing in to the on-premises network
MS Defender for Identity
Suspend user in Entra ID
This will temporarily prevent an Entra ID user from signing in.
MS Defender for Identity
Clear session cookie in Entra ID
Clear a session cookie of an Entra ID user
MS Defender for Identity
Soft Delete Mail
Soft delete mails from a user’s mailbox by moving in into the “deletions” folder
MS Defender for Office
Confirm User Compromised
Marking the user as ‘high risk’ activating the Risky user policy. The user is prompted to securely change their password.
MS Defender for Identity
Indicator Responses
Block Process
Block PROCESS in environment
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Block Hash
Block HASH in environment
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Block IP
Block IP in environment
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
Block Domain / URL
Block domain / URL in environment
MS Defender for Endpoint
MS Defender for Server
MS Defender for Office
Block OAuth App
Block access to unauthorized OAuth app
MS Defender for Cloud Apps
The pre-approved containment action(s) and relevant scope will be formalized in the Customer Scoping document .
cyber security advisory:
Service meetings are standard included monthly unless explicitly mentioned otherwise. During monthly Service meetings, the Cyber Security Advisor will present security Service reporting.
Service Meetings will be held during Business hours unless explicitly agreed otherwise.
The Service Reporting contains all insights regarding the Managed Detection & Response Service during that month.
The following insights will be reported:
The Horizon platform is a central digital engagement point for customers which can be consulted via the user-friendly web interface.
The Horizon Portal can be used for the following activities:
This platform will continuously be improved and developed with more features.
The Cyber Security Advisor is the point of contact for all tactical and strategic security topics and acts as a liaison between Customer’s security stakeholders and Cegeka.
The Cyber Security Advisor has the following responsibilities:
The Cyber Security Advisory is delivered based on an agreed number of days per month, including monthly security Service meetings, all activities required to prepare the meetings and the actions related to decisions agreed upon during the meetings.
platform management:
The licenses for the technologies are not part of the Service itself but can be provided by Cegeka if required.
The goal of Configuration & Release Management is to ensure the quality and performance of the technology used within the Managed Detection & Response service.
Cegeka is responsible for the following activities regarding Configuration & Release Management:
The goal of Health Monitoring is to continuously monitor the health of the technology used within the Managed Detection & Response service.
Cegeka is responsible for the following activities regarding Health Monitoring:
For Cegeka to able to effectively perform Health Monitoring, the customer is responsible for:
A SIEM [2] collects and securely stores the machine data generated from Log Source types such as network, endpoint, identity & access management, anti-malware, and other. The data can be collected using syslog, forwarding agents, API, or other types of integration with data sources and event collectors. Once data has been collected, it will be normalized and correlated against threat detection rules which trigger Security Alerts.
Cegeka is responsible for the following activities regarding Log Management:
In case of a Managed Log Collector, Cegeka is responsible for the additional activities regarding Log Management:
For Cegeka to able to effectively perform Log Management, the customer is responsible for:
The onboarding of new data source types which are not in scope, requires an initial investigation and a separate implementation project on a T&M basis.

process:
For security reasons, only people identified as Authorized Contact are allowed to collaborate with our Cegeka SOC. Only they can interact with the Cegeka SOC. All other persons will automatically be prevented and will be notified towards the Customer. These are also the people who will receive the monthly report.
The Authorized Contacts will be formalized in the Customer Scoping document .
Any new Security Incident will result in a notification towards the customer Operational Contact (s).
If any blocking issues prevents Cegeka from continuing the Service as agreed, an escalation towards the customer Escalation Contact will be done.
The Operational & Escalation contact(s) will be formalized in the Customer Scoping document .

availability:
This Service Level is applicable to all technologies.
Definition
The Availability is determined by the ability of Cegeka’s SOC platform to process new and existing security alerts/incidents. This is not the availability of the supported customers technologies.

time to triage:
This Service Level is applicable to all technologies.
Definition
The amount of time measured from the time a Security Alert is created in our SOC platform to the time a SOC analyst is assigned and Triage will start.
Calculation
T0 : the time a Security Alert is generated in our SOC platform.
T1 : the time a SOC analyst is assigned to perform Triage.
Assumptions
Frequency
Per Month
Objective (SLA)
90%
P1
30 min
P2
30 min
P3
30 min during Business Hours
P4
N/A
time to investigate:
This Service Level is applicable to all technologies.
Definition
The amount of time measured from the time a Security Alert is created in our SOC platform to the time a Security Incident is created, and in-depth investigation will start.

T0 : the time a Security Alert is generated in our SOC platform
T2 : the time a Security Incident is created, and in-depth investigation will start.

R esponsible: The party who is responsible for the execution of the activity.
